properties.name: Rome
properties.title: Treaties of Rome
properties.description: The Treaties of Rome established the European Economic Community and Euratom, turning sectoral cooperation into a broader economic and institutional project.

properties.name: Schengen
properties.title: Schengen Agreement
properties.description: The Schengen Agreement became one of the clearest territorial symbols of European integration: borders did not disappear, but their daily function changed.

properties.name: Maastricht
properties.title: Maastricht Treaty
properties.description: The Maastricht Treaty created the European Union and introduced a new political scale: citizenship, monetary union and a more complex institutional architecture.

properties.name: Brussels
properties.title: Commission and Council
properties.description: Brussels became the most visible administrative centre of the European project: a city where negotiation, regulation and implementation meet.

properties.name: Strasbourg
properties.title: European Parliament
properties.description: Strasbourg connects the European Parliament to the post-war geography of reconciliation and to the long institutional history of European assembly politics.

properties.name: Luxembourg
properties.title: Court of Justice and institutional core
properties.description: Luxembourg represents the legal and institutional depth of integration: European decisions are not only political agreements, but also legal structures.
